title: Installing podman on Ubuntu Xenial has unmet dependencies.
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Installing podman on Ubuntu Xenial has unmet dependencies/ resolving requires removing kubelet/kubernetes-cni packages

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
# docker run -it ubuntu:xenial bash
># apt update
># apt install -y software-properties-common uidmap
># apt install -y apt-transport-https curl
># curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | apt-key add -
># cat <<EOF >/etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
> deb http://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main
> EOF
># apt update
># apt install kubelet
># echo "deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu xenial main" >  /etc/apt/sources.list.d/projectatomic-ppa-xenial.list

># apt update -qq
W: GPG error: http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu xenial InRelease: The following signatures couldn't be verified because the public key is not available: NO_PUBKEY <KEY>
E: The repository 'http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu xenial InRelease' is not signed.
root@d11e9ef22c0b:/# apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys <KEY>

># apt update -qq
># # apt install podman
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree
Reading state information... Done
Some packages could not be installed. This may mean that you have
requested an impossible situation or if you are using the unstable
distribution that some required packages have not yet been created
or been moved out of Incoming.
The following information may help to resolve the situation:

The following packages have unmet dependencies:  < --------------------------------------------
 podman : Depends: containernetworking-plugins (>= 0.7.5) but it is not going to be installed
E: Unable to correct problems, you have held broken packages.
># # apt install containernetworking-plugins
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree
Reading state information... Done
The following packages were automatically installed and are no longer required:
  conntrack ebtables ethtool iproute2 iptables kmod libatm1 libmnl0 libnetfilter-conntrack3 libnfnetlink0 libwrap0 libxtables11 socat tcpd
Use 'apt autoremove' to remove them.
The following packages will be REMOVED: < ------------------------------------------
  kubelet kubernetes-cni
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  containernetworking-plugins
0 upgraded, 1 newly installed, 2 to remove and 5 not upgraded.
Need to get 5615 kB of archives.
After this operation, 130 MB disk space will be freed.
Do you want to continue? [Y/n]
```

**Describe the results you received:**
`apt install podman` has unmet dependencies `containernetworking-plugins` (>= 0.7.5)
installing `containernetworking-plugins` (>= 0.7.5) causes removing kubelet/kubernetes-cni packages


**Describe the results you expected:**
`apt install podman` should be completed without any errors

comments.body:
Same here. On this system:

```
uname -a
Linux master 4.4.0-157-generic #185-Ubuntu SMP Tue Jul 23 09:17:01 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

Using these commands:

```
sudo apt update
sudo apt -y  install software-properties-common
sudo add-apt-repository -y ppa:projectatomic/ppa
sudo apt update
sudo apt -y install podman
```

I get the same message:

```
$ sudo apt -y install podman
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree
Reading state information... Done
Some packages could not be installed. This may mean that you have
requested an impossible situation or if you are using the unstable
distribution that some required packages have not yet been created
or been moved out of Incoming.
The following information may help to resolve the situation:

The following packages have unmet dependencies.
 podman : Depends: containernetworking-plugins (>= 0.7.5) but it is not going to be installed
E: Unable to correct problems, you have held broken packages.
```
And confirm adding 
```
apt install containernetworking-plugins
```
before the podman install is a workaround.
@javajon that is not so good workaround because installing `containernetworking-plugins` on the working kubernetes node requires deleting `kubelet` and `kubernetes-cni` (/opt/cni/bin content)
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

18.04 (x86_64 only) repo does exist on OBS (18.10 however has been EOL'd). 16.04 on OBS looks too expensive to maintain (main issue being an older golang) but in case we have any volunteers toward that, I can work with them.
